The {{nihongo|'''Tomita Chausuyama Kofun'''|富田茶臼山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Ōkawamachi Tomidanaka neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], on the island of [[Shikoku]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1993.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162308|title=富田茶臼山古墳|trans-title= Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middle of the 5th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Tomita Chausuyama Kofun is located on the edge of a low hill on the eastern edge of the Nagao Plain in eastern Kagawa Prefecture. The Tsuda River flows to the north of the Kofun, meandering east and into Tsuda Bay, which opens into the Gulf of Harima on the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. Tsuda Port at the mouth of the river has been known since ancient times and there are many early Kofuns on the hills overlooking Tsuda Bay. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 139 meters and is orientated to the west. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers, and some 3000 ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the second and third tiers only. Numerous fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and house-shaped have been found on the surface. A shield-shaped moat with width of 13 to 20 meters surrounds the mound, bringing the total length including the moat to 163 meters. However, the shape of the mound had been considerably defaced. A [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] was built on top of the mound, and a Buddhist chapel on the east side of the posterior circular portion. The northern portion of the mound was truncated by the construction of Kagawa Prefectural Road No.10 and other parts of the mound were modified due to land clearing and urban encroachment. Thus far, [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted in 1989, and in the surrounding area in 1993-1996; however, the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been excavated, and details are unknown. Local records indicate that the ceiling stone of the burial chamber was dug out in an attempt to build a [[:en:sumo ring|sumo ring]] on the top of the round part of the mound in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the ''haniwa'' and construction methods, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the first half of the middle Kofun period, around the first half of the 5th century.
In subsequent investigations, three additional {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square tumuli were discovered on the southwest and west sides of the Kofun. All three are from the first half of the 5th century and all have with moats surrounding them. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the tumulus was believed to have been variously the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] or his son Prince Nanba, or the tomb of the Sanuki ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' Prince Kamukushi.
The tumulus is about 25 minutes by car from [[:en:Sanuki-Tsuda Station|Sanuki-Tsuda Station]] on the [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Kōtoku Line|Kōtoku Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 139 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 90 meter diameter x 15 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 77 meters wide x 12.5 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 56 meters
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Tomita Chausuyama Kofun'''|富田茶臼山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Ōkawamachi Tomidanaka neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], on the island of [[Shikoku]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1993.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162308|title=富田茶臼山古墳|trans-title= Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middle of the 5th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Tomita Chausuyama Kofun is located on the edge of a low hill on the eastern edge of the Nagao Plain in eastern Kagawa Prefecture. The Tsuda River flows to the north of the Kofun, meandering east and into Tsuda Bay, which opens into the Gulf of Harima on the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. Tsuda Port at the mouth of the river has been known since ancient times and there are many early Kofuns on the hills overlooking Tsuda Bay. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 139 meters and is orientated to the west. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers, and some 3000 ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the second and third tiers only. Numerous fragments of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and house-shaped have been found on the surface. A shield-shaped moat with width of 13 to 20 meters surrounds the mound, bringing the total length including the moat to 163 meters. However, the shape of the mound had been considerably defaced. A [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] was built on top of the mound, and a Buddhist chapel on the east side of the posterior circular portion. The northern portion of the mound was truncated by the construction of Kagawa Prefectural Road No.10 and other parts of the mound were modified due to land clearing and urban encroachment. Thus far, [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted in 1989, and in the surrounding area in 1993-1996; however, the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been excavated, and details are unknown. Local records indicate that the ceiling stone of the burial chamber was dug out in an attempt to build a [[:en:sumo ring|sumo ring]] on the top of the round part of the mound in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the ''haniwa'' and construction methods, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the first half of the middle Kofun period, around the first half of the 5th century.
In subsequent investigations, three additional {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square tumuli were discovered on the southwest and west sides of the Kofun. All three are from the first half of the 5th century and all have with moats surrounding them. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the tumulus was believed to have been variously the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] or his son Prince Nanba, or the tomb of the Sanuki ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' Prince Kamukushi.
The tumulus is about 25 minutes by car from [[:en:Sanuki-Tsuda Station|Sanuki-Tsuda Station]] on the [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Kōtoku Line|Kōtoku Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 139 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 90 meter diameter x 15 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 77 meters wide x 12.5 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 56 meters
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)]]

circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|15|35.86|N|134|14|38.39|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tomita Chausuyama Kofun'''|富田茶臼山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Ōkawamachi Tomidanaka neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1993.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162308|title=富田茶臼山古墳|trans-title= Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middle of the 5th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Tomita Chausuyama Kofun is located on the edge of a low hill on the eastern edge of the Nagao Plain in eastern Kagawa Prefecture. The Tsuda River flows to the north of the Kofun, meandering east and into Tsuda Bay, which opens into the Gulf of Harima on the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. Tsuda Port at the mouth of the river has been known since ancient times and there are many early Kofuns on the hills overlooking Tsuda Bay. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 139 meters and is orientated to the west. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers, and some 3000 ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the second and third tiers only. Numerous fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and house-shaped have been found on the surface. A shield-shaped moat with width of 13 to 20 meters surrounds the mound, bringing the total length including the moat to 163 meters. However, the shape of the mound had been considerably defaced. A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} was built on top of the mound, and a Buddhist chapel on the east side of the posterior circular portion. The northern portion of the mound was truncated by the construction of Kagawa Prefectural Road No.10 and other parts of the mound were modified due to land clearing and urban encroachment. Thus far, [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted in 1989, and in the surrounding area in 1993-1996; however, the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been excavated, and details are unknown. Local records indicate that the ceiling stone of the burial chamber was dug out in an attempt to build a [[:en:sumo ring|sumo ring]] on the top of the round part of the mound in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the ''haniwa'' and construction methods, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the first half of the middle Kofun period, around the first half of the 5th century.
In subsequent investigations, three additional {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square tumuli were discovered on the southwest and west sides of the Kofun. All three are from the first half of the 5th century and all have with moats surrounding them. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the tumulus was believed to have been variously the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} or his son Prince Nanba, or the tomb of the Sanuki ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' Prince Kamukushi.
The tumulus is about 25 minutes by car from {{ill|Sanuki-Tsuda Station|en|Sanuki-Tsuda Station|WD=Q7420743|lt=Sanuki-Tsuda Station}} on the [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] {{ill|Kōtoku Line|en|Kōtoku Line|WD=Q859984|lt=Kōtoku Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 139 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 90 meter diameter x 15 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 77 meters wide x 12.5 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 56 meters
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[https://www.kochi-maibun.jp/lectures_detail89.html Kochi Prefecture Archaeological Center home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Tomita Chausuyama Kofun'''|富田茶臼山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Ōkawamachi Tomidanaka neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1993.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162308|title=富田茶臼山古墳|trans-title= Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middle of the 5th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Tomita Chausuyama Kofun is located on the edge of a low hill on the eastern edge of the Nagao Plain in eastern Kagawa Prefecture. The Tsuda River flows to the north of the Kofun, meandering east and into Tsuda Bay, which opens into the Gulf of Harima on the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. Tsuda Port at the mouth of the river has been known since ancient times and there are many early Kofuns on the hills overlooking Tsuda Bay. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 139 meters and is orientated to the west. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers, and some 3000 ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the second and third tiers only. Numerous fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and house-shaped have been found on the surface. A shield-shaped moat with width of 13 to 20 meters surrounds the mound, bringing the total length including the moat to 163 meters. However, the shape of the mound had been considerably defaced. A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} was built on top of the mound, and a Buddhist chapel on the east side of the posterior circular portion. The northern portion of the mound was truncated by the construction of Kagawa Prefectural Road No.10 and other parts of the mound were modified due to land clearing and urban encroachment. Thus far, [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted in 1989, and in the surrounding area in 1993-1996; however, the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been excavated, and details are unknown. Local records indicate that the ceiling stone of the burial chamber was dug out in an attempt to build a [[:en:sumo ring|sumo ring]] on the top of the round part of the mound in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the ''haniwa'' and construction methods, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the first half of the middle Kofun period, around the first half of the 5th century.
In subsequent investigations, three additional {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square tumuli were discovered on the southwest and west sides of the Kofun. All three are from the first half of the 5th century and all have with moats surrounding them. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the tumulus was believed to have been variously the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} or his son Prince Nanba, or the tomb of the Sanuki ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' Prince Kamukushi.
The tumulus is about 25 minutes by car from {{ill|Sanuki-Tsuda Station|en|Sanuki-Tsuda Station|WD=Q7420743|lt=Sanuki-Tsuda Station}} on the [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] {{ill|Kōtoku Line|en|Kōtoku Line|WD=Q859984|lt=Kōtoku Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 139 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 90 meter diameter x 15 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 77 meters wide x 12.5 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 56 meters

270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.26|N|134.24416667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|15|35.86|N|134|14|38.39|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tomita Chausuyama Kofun'''|富田茶臼山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Ōkawamachi Tomidanaka neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1993.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162308|title=富田茶臼山古墳|trans-title= Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middle of the 5th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Tomita Chausuyama Kofun is located on the edge of a low hill on the eastern edge of the Nagao Plain in eastern Kagawa Prefecture. The Tsuda River flows to the north of the Kofun, meandering east and into Tsuda Bay, which opens into the Gulf of Harima on the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. Tsuda Port at the mouth of the river has been known since ancient times and there are many early Kofuns on the hills overlooking Tsuda Bay. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 139 meters and is orientated to the west. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers, and some 3000 ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the second and third tiers only. Numerous fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and house-shaped have been found on the surface. A shield-shaped moat with width of 13 to 20 meters surrounds the mound, bringing the total length including the moat to 163 meters. However, the shape of the mound had been considerably defaced. A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} was built on top of the mound, and a Buddhist chapel on the east side of the posterior circular portion. The northern portion of the mound was truncated by the construction of Kagawa Prefectural Road No.10 and other parts of the mound were modified due to land clearing and urban encroachment. Thus far, [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted in 1989, and in the surrounding area in 1993-1996; however, the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been excavated, and details are unknown. Local records indicate that the ceiling stone of the burial chamber was dug out in an attempt to build a [[:en:sumo ring|sumo ring]] on the top of the round part of the mound in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the ''haniwa'' and construction methods, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the first half of the middle Kofun period, around the first half of the 5th century.
In subsequent investigations, three additional {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square tumuli were discovered on the southwest and west sides of the Kofun. All three are from the first half of the 5th century and all have with moats surrounding them. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the tumulus was believed to have been variously the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} or his son Prince Nanba, or the tomb of the Sanuki ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' Prince Kamukushi.
The tumulus is about 25 minutes by car from {{ill|Sanuki-Tsuda Station|en|Sanuki-Tsuda Station|WD=Q7420743|lt=Sanuki-Tsuda Station}} on the [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] {{ill|Kōtoku Line|en|Kōtoku Line|WD=Q859984|lt=Kōtoku Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 139 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 90 meter diameter x 15 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 77 meters wide x 12.5 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 56 meters
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Sanuki, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tomita Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =富田茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tomida Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomita Chausuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomita Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.26|N|134.24416667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|15|35.86|N|134|14|38.39|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tomita Chausuyama Kofun'''|富田茶臼山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Ōkawamachi Tomidanaka neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1993.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162308|title=富田茶臼山古墳|trans-title= Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middle of the 5th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Tomita Chausuyama Kofun is located on the edge of a low hill on the eastern edge of the Nagao Plain in eastern Kagawa Prefecture. The Tsuda River flows to the north of the Kofun, meandering east and into Tsuda Bay, which opens into the Gulf of Harima on the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. Tsuda Port at the mouth of the river has been known since ancient times and there are many early Kofuns on the hills overlooking Tsuda Bay. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 139 meters and is orientated to the west. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers, and some 3000 ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the second and third tiers only. Numerous fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and house-shaped have been found on the surface. A shield-shaped moat with width of 13 to 20 meters surrounds the mound, bringing the total length including the moat to 163 meters. However, the shape of the mound had been considerably defaced. A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} was built on top of the mound, and a Buddhist chapel on the east side of the posterior circular portion. The northern portion of the mound was truncated by the construction of Kagawa Prefectural Road No.10 and other parts of the mound were modified due to land clearing and urban encroachment. Thus far, [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted in 1989, and in the surrounding area in 1993-1996; however, the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been excavated, and details are unknown. Local records indicate that the ceiling stone of the burial chamber was dug out in an attempt to build a [[:en:sumo ring|sumo ring]] on the top of the round part of the mound in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the ''haniwa'' and construction methods, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the first half of the middle Kofun period, around the first half of the 5th century.
In subsequent investigations, three additional {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square tumuli were discovered on the southwest and west sides of the Kofun. All three are from the first half of the 5th century and all have with moats surrounding them. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the tumulus was believed to have been variously the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} or his son Prince Nanba, or the tomb of the Sanuki ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' Prince Kamukushi.
The tumulus is about 25 minutes by car from {{ill|Sanuki-Tsuda Station|en|Sanuki-Tsuda Station|WD=Q7420743|lt=Sanuki-Tsuda Station}} on the [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] {{ill|Kōtoku Line|en|Kōtoku Line|WD=Q859984|lt=Kōtoku Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 139 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 90 meter diameter x 15 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 77 meters wide x 12.5 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 56 meters
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]]{{template-inline}} *[https://www.kochi-maibun.jp/lectures_detail89.html Kochi Prefecture Archaeological Center home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Sanuki, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tomita Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =富田茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tomida Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomita Chausuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomita Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.26|N|134.24416667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki, Kagawa|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|15|35.86|N|134|14|38.39|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tomita Chausuyama Kofun'''|富田茶臼山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Ōkawamachi Tomidanaka neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1993.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162308|title=富田茶臼山古墳|trans-title= Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middle of the 5th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Tomita Chausuyama Kofun is located on the edge of a low hill on the eastern edge of the Nagao Plain in eastern Kagawa Prefecture. The Tsuda River flows to the north of the Kofun, meandering east and into Tsuda Bay, which opens into the Gulf of Harima on the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. Tsuda Port at the mouth of the river has been known since ancient times and there are many early Kofuns on the hills overlooking Tsuda Bay. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 139 meters and is orientated to the west. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers, and some 3000 ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the second and third tiers only. Numerous fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and house-shaped have been found on the surface. A shield-shaped moat with width of 13 to 20 meters surrounds the mound, bringing the total length including the moat to 163 meters. However, the shape of the mound had been considerably defaced. A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} was built on top of the mound, and a Buddhist chapel on the east side of the posterior circular portion. The northern portion of the mound was truncated by the construction of Kagawa Prefectural Road No.10 and other parts of the mound were modified due to land clearing and urban encroachment. Thus far, [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted in 1989, and in the surrounding area in 1993-1996; however, the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been excavated, and details are unknown. Local records indicate that the ceiling stone of the burial chamber was dug out in an attempt to build a [[:en:sumo ring|sumo ring]] on the top of the round part of the mound in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the ''haniwa'' and construction methods, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the first half of the middle Kofun period, around the first half of the 5th century.
In subsequent investigations, three additional {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square tumuli were discovered on the southwest and west sides of the Kofun. All three are from the first half of the 5th century and all have with moats surrounding them. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the tumulus was believed to have been variously the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} or his son Prince Nanba, or the tomb of the Sanuki ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' Prince Kamukushi.
The tumulus is about 25 minutes by car from {{ill|Sanuki-Tsuda Station|en|Sanuki-Tsuda Station|WD=Q7420743|lt=Sanuki-Tsuda Station|1=Sanuki-Tsuda Station}} on the [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] {{ill|Kōtoku Line|en|Kōtoku Line|WD=Q859984|lt=Kōtoku Line|1=Kōtoku Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 139 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 90 meter diameter x 15 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 77 meters wide x 12.5 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 56 meters
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tomida Chausuyama Kofun]][[en:Tomida Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:富田茶臼山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tomida Chausuyama Kofun]][[en:Tomida Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:富田茶臼山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tomida Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:富田茶臼山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Sanuki, Kagawa, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Tomita Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =富田茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Tomida Chausuyama Kofun, zenkei-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tomita Chausuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Kagawa Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tomita Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.26|N|134.24416667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki, Kagawa|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Shikoku]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|15|35.86|N|134|14|38.39|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tomita Chausuyama Kofun'''|富田茶臼山古墳|}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Ōkawamachi Tomidanaka neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki|1=Sanuki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}, on the island of [[Shikoku]] of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1993.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162308|title=富田茶臼山古墳|trans-title= Tomita Chausuyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in the Shikoku region, and is estimated to have been built around the middle of the 5th century (the latter half of the early [[Kofun period]]).
==Overview== The Tomita Chausuyama Kofun is located on the edge of a low hill on the eastern edge of the Nagao Plain in eastern Kagawa Prefecture. The Tsuda River flows to the north of the Kofun, meandering east and into Tsuda Bay, which opens into the Gulf of Harima on the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. Tsuda Port at the mouth of the river has been known since ancient times and there are many early Kofuns on the hills overlooking Tsuda Bay. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 139 meters and is orientated to the west. The tumulus was constructed in three tiers, and some 3000 ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found on the second and third tiers only. Numerous fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', including cylindrical, morning glory-shaped, and house-shaped have been found on the surface. A shield-shaped moat with width of 13 to 20 meters surrounds the mound, bringing the total length including the moat to 163 meters. However, the shape of the mound had been considerably defaced. A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} was built on top of the mound, and a Buddhist chapel on the east side of the posterior circular portion. The northern portion of the mound was truncated by the construction of Kagawa Prefectural Road No.10 and other parts of the mound were modified due to land clearing and urban encroachment. Thus far, [[archaeological excavation]]s have been conducted in 1989, and in the surrounding area in 1993-1996; however, the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been excavated, and details are unknown. Local records indicate that the ceiling stone of the burial chamber was dug out in an attempt to build a [[:en:sumo ring|sumo ring]] on the top of the round part of the mound in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the ''haniwa'' and construction methods, the tumulus is estimated to have been built in the first half of the middle Kofun period, around the first half of the 5th century.
In subsequent investigations, three additional {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} square tumuli were discovered on the southwest and west sides of the Kofun. All three are from the first half of the 5th century and all have with moats surrounding them. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
In the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the tumulus was believed to have been variously the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} or his son Prince Nanba, or the tomb of the Sanuki ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' Prince Kamukushi.
The tumulus is about 25 minutes by car from {{ill|Sanuki-Tsuda Station|en|Sanuki-Tsuda Station|WD=Q7420743|lt=Sanuki-Tsuda Station|1=Sanuki-Tsuda Station}} on the [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] {{ill|Kōtoku Line|en|Kōtoku Line|WD=Q859984|lt=Kōtoku Line|1=Kōtoku Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 139 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 90 meter diameter x 15 meter high x 3 tiers ;Anterior rectangular portion: 77 meters wide x 12.5 meters high x 3 tiers ;Constriction width: 56 meters
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|WD=Q6573268|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kagawa)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]]{{template-inline}} *[https://www.kochi-maibun.jp/lectures_detail89.html Kochi Prefecture Archaeological Center home page] {{in lang|ja}}
